meaning: adj.特殊物质的,特殊的,异质的
examples: She still appeared to be an idiosyncratic figure.
His teaching methods are idiosyncratic but successful.
The speaking voice, by contrast, is something idiosyncratic, irregular, particular.
Still, the results are relatively idiosyncratic, including jewelry shown on dogs and womenswear shown on men.
Remember that you are just one consumer and likely a slightly idiosyncratic one at that.
